channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: Doctor evacuated from Congo feels ‘helpless’ watching colleagues die of Ebola
item.description: Surrounded by a plastic bubble, Patrick LaRochelle spoke to The Post as he waits in a specialized hospital room in Prague to see whether he has Ebola.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Son can serve his country but can’t acknowledge parents’ birthdays
item.description: Their son still visits but ignores the family’s special days. Is this a sign of some deep-seated resentment?

item.title: Trump administration proposes nondisclosure agreements for all federal workers in effort to crack down on media leaks
item.description: The planned rule would bar federal workers from sharing “confidential government information,” applying an expansive definition of privileged information. The rule would expand NDAs instituted at the Pentagon and other agencies.

item.title: Do Russians support the Ukraine war? This poll is remarkable.
item.description: Repressions mount as Russians turn against the Ukraine conflict.

item.title: Federal court blocks redistricting map created to help Republicans in Alabama
item.description: The court's decision delivers a win to Democrats. The new voting map had been designed to give Republicans an edge in several congressional districts for November's elections. The decision could be appealed.

item.title: A clothing ‘betrayal’ in today’s On the Record
item.description: A clothing ‘betrayal’ in today’s On the Record

item.title: Tehran threatens retaliation after U.S. strikes in southern Iran
item.description: The attacks prompted Iran to warn that it would “leave no act of hostility unanswered,” complicating negotiations to end the war.

item.title: Iran strikes. Texas runoff. Trump’s ballroom rage. AI warning. Ebola doctor. Sleep sweet spot. Enhanced Games. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: U.S. renews strikes on Iran, citing threats posed to American troops
item.description: The military said the strikes were conducted in southern Iran “to protect our troops from threats posed by Iranian forces.” Targets included missile-launch sites and Iranian boats attempting to set mines, the military said in a statement. The strikes occurred after a weekend of negotiations between the Trump administration and the Iranian regime to bring the war to a close.

item.title: President Donald Trump’s unofficial Venezuela viceroy shapes U.S. policy, raising oversight concerns
item.description: Mauricio Claver-Carone doesn’t work for the U.S. government but has an outsize role in determining the future of Venezuela.

item.title: This is the reality of European-style social spending
item.description: America’s tax wedge is the lowest of all advanced economies in the Group of Seven.

item.title: Here’s what antibiotics do to your gut — and what you can do about it
item.description: Our columnist, gastroenterologist Trisha Pasricha, recommends that you skip the probiotic with your antibiotics and bolster your microbiome with these three strategies instead.

item.title: Trump seeks to widen Abraham Accords as new Iran deal faces sharp criticism
item.description: In the latest twist to his bid to end the war against Iran, President Donald Trump on Monday urged several majority-Muslim countries to join the Abraham Accords, a set of treaties aimed at normalizing relations with Israel, as part of the settlement he is seeking to negotiate with Tehran.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Partner expects nightlife to go on (and on), even after baby
item.description: Carolyn Hax: Partner expects nightlife to go on (and on), even after baby

item.title: Some of Texas’s oldest barbecue joints close as meat prices skyrocket
item.description: Even the state’s most celebrated restaurants are struggling to remain open while costs climb with no relief in sight.

item.title: What I saw in Texas as its vicious GOP Senate runoff nears the finish
item.description: The Trump-endorsed Ken Paxton leads Senate veteran John Cornyn.

item.title: Know your summer movies in today’s On the Record
item.description: Know your summer movies in today’s On the Record

item.title: Trump faces health questions ahead of another Walter Reed trip
item.description: The president trumpets claims that he is in excellent shape, but independent physicians say the White House hasn’t answered key questions ahead of his third visit in 13 months.

item.title: Iran talks. White House shooting. Trump’s payout fund. Gas prices. Diplomatic homes. Gut health. Grilling recipes. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Trump’s Iran exit ramp is a long shot. He doesn’t have a better option.
item.description: The administration's peace framework asks the mullahs to choose prosperity over ideology.

item.title: Tech billionaires used performance drugs in secret. Now they’re selling a revolution through the Enhanced Games.
item.description: As biohacking gains momentum among elites, the Enhanced Games aim to normalize longevity drugs through a Las Vegas sports spectacle. Critics call the premise dangerous and unethical.

item.title: Gunman who was killed near White House had cut off contact with friends, claimed he was Jesus
item.description: The suspect’s life seemed to take one wrong turn after another in recent years, according to court records and a high school friend.

item.title: Trump said gas prices are ‘peanuts.’ We calculated how the increases have impacted different income groups.
item.description: Surging gas prices have hit American drivers hard — but some much harder than others, according to a Washington Post analysis.

item.title: As war evolves, more female troops make the ultimate sacrifice
item.description: This Memorial Day, military families are remembering the Iran conflict’s fallen and navigating heartbreak without mothers and wives.

item.title: Still need to finish this weekend’s On the Record? We’ve got you.
item.description: Play now to finish out your score for the week.

item.title: A suspicious decline in birth rates points to a new culprit
item.description: The infinite scroll could contribute to lower fertility rates and smaller graduating classes.

item.title: How scientists found a weakness in one of the the deadliest ‘undruggable’ cancers
item.description: An experimental pill has shown unprecedented success in treating pancreatic cancer, upending conventional wisdom about the disease.

item.title: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now
item.description: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now

item.title: Gunman dead, bystander wounded after shots exchanged near White House, Secret Service says
item.description: The federal law enforcement agency said an individual approached a checkpoint just outside the White House complex and started shooting at posted officers. The Secret Service returned fire, fatally striking the suspect, who died at a hospital. A bystander was also injured in the exchange, according to the Secret Service, which did not detail a motivation for the shooting.

item.title: Gunshots fired near the White House, FBI director says
item.description: “FBI is on the scene and supporting Secret Service responding to shots fired near White House grounds,” FBI Director Kash Patel said on social media. President Donald Trump has been at the White House throughout the day, making calls from the Oval Office.

item.title: 6 money tips new graduates should know, according to our columnist
item.description: There are some basic yet vital things young adults should know to keep their debt low and their net worth growing, Michelle Singletary says.

item.title: Trump says Iran ceasefire deal in final stages, to be ‘announced shortly’
item.description: The president said on social media that the progress was made after calls with several leaders in the Middle East.

item.title: The simple trick that can stop a mosquito bite from itching
item.description: Experts explain how to safely apply heat or cold to curb the urge to scratch an itchy mosquito bite. For milder mosquito bites, over-the-counter options can offer some relief, as will some lotions.

item.title: Claws out as wildlife center, chicken joint owner brawl over planned expansion
item.description: Stan Seymour, who owns 14 Bojangles restaurants in Virginia, and the Southwest Virginia Wildlife Center have been engaged in a protracted legal battle since the center sought to build an aviary for raptors. Combined, the longtime neighbors have spent about $700,000 in their fight.

item.title: Trump’s $1.8 billion payout fund isn’t required to reveal how it actually works
item.description: A five-member panel would distribute the money — compensation for people claiming harm by the justice system — largely shielded from public scrutiny, outside of any evident oversight, and answerable only to the president and his appointees.

item.title: People with eating disorders are taking GLP-1s, and doctors are alarmed
item.description: Providers said they are routinely encountering such patients, some of whom have suffered a relapse and others who have developed an eating disorder for the first time while on the medications.

item.title: Democrats’ rearview mirror is cracked. So is the windshield.
item.description: The autopsy on the 2024 campaign reflects the party's continuing misreading of America.

item.title: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.
item.description: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.

item.title: The 32 most anticipated movies of the summer
item.description: Spider-Man and the Minions are back, but this season might belong to Odysseus and the aliens.

item.title: Will the Ukraine war force Putin’s exit? David Ignatius explains.
item.description: Adam O’Neal sits down with David Ignatius to discuss how the Ukraine war is going.

item.title: Judge dismisses criminal case against Kilmar Abrego García, calling it 'an abuse of prosecuting power'
item.description: The Maryland man was charged with human smuggling after a high-profile legal fight in which courts ruled he had been illegally deported to El Salvador.

item.title: New rule requires most green-card applicants to apply from outside U.S.
item.description: Immigration lawyers say the change could affect a wide swath of people and marks an escalation in the administration’s efforts to curtail legal migration.

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as director of national intelligence
item.description: In a statement posted online, Gabbard said she was leaving the post due to her husband’s diagnosis of a rare bone cancer.

item.title: Carolyn Hax chat: My girlfriend refuses to pay me back. Should I break up with her?
item.description: Our advice columnist answers your questions about the strange train we call life.

item.title: Cancer may take my life. Here’s what a hymn made me see.
item.description: A grim prognosis strengthened my belief in providence.

item.title: D.C.’s top baby name picks are revealed, and they lean socially conservative
item.description: The Social Security Administration released data for last year’s most popular names. Baby naming experts weighed in on the city’s picks.

item.title: Wager your points and finish off the week with today’s On the Record.
item.description: Wager your points and finish off the week with today’s On the Record.

item.title: Everything that happened on Stephen Colbert’s star-studded final episode of ‘The Late Show’
item.description: The comedian wrapped up the CBS franchise with surprise celebrity guests, a visit from his fellow late-night hosts and multiple songs.

item.title: Economic confidence is plunging, poll finds, a potential midterm problem for Trump
item.description: Americans’ confidence in the economy has hit a nearly four-year low, threatening Republicans ahead of the midterm elections as President Donald Trump struggles to deliver on affordability pledges.

item.title: Republican blowup. AI order. Kamala Harris. Ebola flight. Wet weekend. Colbert’s send-off. Summer movies. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.
